title: Assessment priorities for airway anatomy
short_summary: Learn how to identify, prioritize, and manage airway anatomy at the EMT level.
why_it_matters: Airway Anatomy matters because poor recognition can delay critical care and transport decisions.
must_know_points: Use scene safety, primary assessment, vital signs, patient story, red flags, reassessment, and local protocol.
red_flags: Severe distress, altered mental status, poor perfusion, hypotension, cyanosis, chest pain, neuro deficit, abnormal breathing.
contraindications_or_dangers: Do not exceed scope; avoid tunnel vision; verify contraindications and reassess after interventions.
common_mistakes: Skipping primary assessment, ignoring trends, treating numbers instead of patient, forgetting pediatric differences.
field_pearls: Make one priority decision at a time; explain why each intervention changes outcome.

why_it_matters: Pediatrics matters because poor recognition can delay critical care and transport decisions.
must_know_points: Use scene safety, primary assessment, vital signs, patient story, red flags, reassessment, and local protocol.
red_flags: Severe distress, altered mental status, poor perfusion, hypotension, cyanosis, chest pain, neuro deficit, abnormal breathing.
contraindications_or_dangers: Do not exceed scope; avoid tunnel vision; verify contraindications and reassess after interventions.
common_mistakes: Skipping primary assessment, ignoring trends, treating numbers instead of patient, forgetting pediatric differences.
field_pearls: Make one priority decision at a time; explain why each intervention changes outcome.

why_it_matters: Oxygen Delivery matters because poor recognition can delay critical care and transport decisions.
must_know_points: Use scene safety, primary assessment, vital signs, patient story, red flags, reassessment, and local protocol.
red_flags: Severe distress, altered mental status, poor perfusion, hypotension, cyanosis, chest pain, neuro deficit, abnormal breathing.
contraindications_or_dangers: Do not exceed scope; avoid tunnel vision; verify contraindications and reassess after interventions.
common_mistakes: Skipping primary assessment, ignoring trends, treating numbers instead of patient, forgetting pediatric differences.
field_pearls: Make one priority decision at a time; explain why each intervention changes outcome.

title: Primary assessment
equipment_needed: PPE, monitor if available, oxygen/airway supplies
critical_fail_points: Skipping life threats; treating before identifying ABC threats
reassessment_points: Reassess ABCs after each critical intervention
documentation_points: Document mental status, airway, breathing, circulation, interventions
_rag_text: skl_000001 | Primary assessment | emt | All patient contacts | Unsafe scene only | PPE, monitor if available, oxygen/airway supplies | BSI, scene safety, general impression | Skipping life threats; treating before identifying ABC threats | Scene safety first | Reassess ABCs after each critical intervention | Document mental status, airway, breathing, circulation, interventions | 2 | draft

title: BVM ventilation
equipment_needed: BVM, mask, oxygen, airway adjunct, suction
preparation_steps: Position airway, seal mask, attach oxygen
critical_fail_points: Overventilation, poor seal, no chest rise
_rag_text: skl_000002 | BVM ventilation | emt | Inadequate breathing or apnea | No absolute contraindication in apnea | BVM, mask, oxygen, airway adjunct, suction | Position airway, seal mask, attach oxygen | Overventilation, poor seal, no chest rise | Avoid gastric inflation | Watch chest rise, compliance, SpO2/ETCO2 | Ventilation rate, response, adjuncts | 3 | draft

title: Bleeding control
contraindications: Do not delay tourniquet for massive bleeding
_rag_text: skl_000003 | Bleeding control | emt | Life-threatening external hemorrhage | Do not delay tourniquet for massive bleeding | Gloves, gauze, hemostatic dressing, tourniquet | Expose, identify source, direct pressure/tourniquet | Tourniquet too loose; delayed application | PPE and sharps safety | Recheck bleeding and distal status | Time tourniquet placed | 2 | draft

title: Spinal motion restriction
indications: Trauma with high-risk features or neuro deficit
preparation_steps: Assess mechanism, neuro, pain, distracting injury
_rag_text: skl_000004 | Spinal motion restriction | emt | Trauma with high-risk features or neuro deficit | No high-risk features after assessment | Collar, stretcher, straps, padding | Assess mechanism, neuro, pain, distracting injury | Immobilizing everyone blindly; airway compromise | Manual stabilization initially | Reassess PMS and airway | SMR indication and neuro findings | 2 | draft

title: Radio report
preparation_steps: Organize age/sex, complaint, vitals, treatment, ETA
_rag_text: skl_000005 | Radio report | emt | Transporting patient needing notification | No contraindication | Radio/phone, notes | Organize age/sex, complaint, vitals, treatment, ETA | Rambling, missing ETA or acuity | Protect privacy | Update if status changes | Report time, receiving facility | 1 | draft

title: IV access
_rag_text: skl_000006 | IV access | aemt | Medication/fluid route indicated | Local contraindications, infection, fistula/limb restriction | IV catheter, saline, dressing, sharps | Choose site, aseptic technique | Failed sharps safety; infiltration ignored | Sharps safety | Check patency and site | Gauge, site, attempts, fluid | 3 | draft

title: Supraglottic airway
critical_fail_points: Not confirming placement; no ventilation assessment
_rag_text: skl_000007 | Supraglottic airway | aemt | Apnea/airway failure per protocol | Gag reflex or device contraindication | SGA, lubricant, BVM, suction | Preoxygenate, insert per device | Not confirming placement; no ventilation assessment | Suction ready | ETCO2/chest rise/lung sounds | Device size, attempts, confirmation | 4 | draft

title: Manual defibrillation
_rag_text: skl_000008 | Manual defibrillation | paramedic | Shockable cardiac arrest | Non-shockable rhythm | Monitor/defib, pads, CPR team | Confirm rhythm, charge, clear, shock | Shocking artifact, unsafe clearing | Clear loudly and visually | Resume CPR immediately | Energy, rhythm, response | 4 | draft

title: Synchronized cardioversion
_rag_text: skl_000009 | Synchronized cardioversion | paramedic | Unstable tachycardia with pulse | Ventricular fibrillation/pulseless VT | Monitor/defib, sedation per protocol | Confirm unstable tachycardia, sync mode | Forgetting sync; delaying unstable patient | Team clear | Reassess rhythm and perfusion | Energy, rhythm, sedation, response | 5 | draft

title: 12-lead acquisition
indications: Chest pain, dyspnea, syncope, suspected ACS
_rag_text: skl_000010 | 12-lead acquisition | paramedic | Chest pain, dyspnea, syncope, suspected ACS | Do not delay life-saving care | Monitor, electrodes, skin prep | Place leads correctly, acquire, transmit | Lead misplacement, artifact | Maintain privacy | Repeat if symptoms change | Time, interpretation, transmission | 3 | draft

title: Medication infusion setup
critical_fail_points: Dose error, unit error, wrong concentration
_rag_text: skl_000011 | Medication infusion setup | paramedic | Medication requiring controlled infusion | Drug-specific contraindications | Medication, pump/tubing, calculator | Confirm dose, concentration, rate | Dose error, unit error, wrong concentration | Double-check high-risk meds | Monitor response and adverse effects | Dose, concentration, route, response | 5 | draft

contraindications: Allergy, active major bleeding, local protocol exclusions
_rag_text: drug_000001 | dc_000004 | Aspirin | ASA | Antiplatelet | emt | Suspected ACS with no contraindication | Allergy, active major bleeding, local protocol exclusions | 160-325 mg chewable per protocol | Generally protocol-dependent; consult local guidance | PO | Usually once prehospital | Minutes | Hours | GI upset, bleeding, allergy | Pain, allergy signs, bleeding risk | Dose/contraindications vary; check local protocol | draft

indications: Chest pain suspected cardiac origin with adequate BP and prescription/protocol
contraindications: Hypotension, PDE-5 inhibitor use, RV infarct concern, local exclusions
repeat_dose_rules: Repeat per protocol with BP checks
_rag_text: drug_000002 | dc_000005 | Nitroglycerin | Nitrostat | Vasodilator | emt | Chest pain suspected cardiac origin with adequate BP and prescription/protocol | Hypotension, PDE-5 inhibitor use, RV infarct concern, local exclusions | 0.4 mg SL per protocol | Typically not pediatric routine prehospital | SL | Repeat per protocol with BP checks | 1-3 min | 20-30 min | Hypotension, headache, dizziness | BP before/after, pain, dizziness | Highly protocol-dependent | draft

contraindications: No absolute contraindication in life-threatening anaphylaxis
_rag_text: drug_000003 | dc_000002 | Epinephrine 1 mg/mL | Adrenalin | Adrenergic | emt | Anaphylaxis via auto-injector or protocol | No absolute contraindication in life-threatening anaphylaxis | Auto-injector dose per device/protocol | Pediatric auto-injector dose per device/protocol | IM | Repeat per protocol | Minutes | Short | Tachycardia, anxiety, tremor | Airway, breathing, BP, response | Dose/device varies | draft

indications: Suspected opioid overdose with respiratory depression
protocol_variation_note: Titrate to breathing, not full alertness where allowed
_rag_text: drug_000004 | dc_000003 | Naloxone | Narcan | Antidote | emt | Suspected opioid overdose with respiratory depression | Hypersensitivity; use caution with dependence | Per protocol intranasal/IM/IV depending level | Per protocol weight/age guidance | IN/IM/IV | Repeat to adequate respirations | 2-5 min depending route | 30-90 min | Withdrawal, vomiting, agitation | Respiratory rate, airway, mental status | Titrate to breathing, not full alertness where allowed | draft

monitoring: Work of breathing, lung sounds, HR
_rag_text: drug_000005 | dc_000002 | Albuterol | Ventolin | Adrenergic bronchodilator | emt | Bronchospasm/wheezing per protocol | Allergy, severe tachyarrhythmia caution | 2.5 mg nebulized commonly; protocol varies | Weight/age protocol dependent | Nebulized | Repeat per protocol | 5-15 min | 3-6 hr | Tachycardia, tremor | Work of breathing, lung sounds, HR | EMT assist/admin rules vary | draft

identification_pearls: Irregularly irregular rhythm without consistent P waves.
